rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component radical 1,2-silyl-oxygenation of electron-deficient alkenes with hydrosilanes and N-hydroxy (N-oxyl) compounds to give β-silyl-oxygenated products.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of N-hydroxyphthalimide (NHPI) to the phthalimido-N-oxyl radical (PINO), which performs HAT on the Si-H bond to generate the silyl radical; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: β-silyl carbon radical (Int-1) from silyl addition to alkene

electron_flow: At the anode, NHPI is oxidized (with proton loss) to PINO, which abstracts H from the hydrosilane to give a silyl radical; the silyl radical adds to the electron-deficient alkene to form a β-silyl C-radical, which is trapped by a second N-oxyl radical to deliver the C-O bond, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3.0 equiv TEMPO completely suppressed the reaction; product 4a not detected, consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane (substrate 9, dimethyl/ethyl ester-substituted)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 10 isolated in 28% yield, supporting a β-silyl C-radical intermediate (Int-1).

probe_reagents.name: NHPI / Ph3SiH (CV study)
probe_reagents.observation: CV showed NHPI has lower oxidation potential than Ph3SiH; a new oxidation peak appeared for the NHPI + Ph3SiH mixture, supporting NHPI-mediated anodic oxidation/HAT.

notes: Mechanism is mediator-based: NHPI/PINO serves as an HAT mediator at the anode to generate silyl radicals from hydrosilanes, avoiding direct silane oxidation. The N-oxyl moiety plays a dual role — anodic mediator and the oxygen-coupling partner that traps the β-silyl C-radical. Cathodic half-reaction is H2 evolution in an undivided cell.
